title: Assertion `(mFlags&(MEM_Str|MEM_Blob))==0 || (pMem->n==pX->n && pMem->z==pX->z)' failed.
description:
yongheng added on 2020-03-03 19:42:18:
POC:
---
CREATE TABLE v0(ALWAYS AS(v3), v3, v5);
INSERT INTO v0(v5) VALUES(7), (1);
UPDATE v0 SET v3 = zeroblob(v3);
---

It tiggers an assertion in debug build of latest trunk.
drh added on 2020-03-03 20:06:37:
This is similar to ticket [d165ad781b39d574].  The assert() is inside of
logic that occurs in debugging and testing builds only.  That logic attempts
to prove the register values are not reused after they become stale.  This
particular assert() is part of a system to help verify that the verification
logic is complete.
No impact on release builds.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table(id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, data BYTEA, value INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0);
INSERT INTO test_table(data, value) VALUES(decode('0000000000', 'hex'), 5), (NULL, NULL);
UPDATE test_table SET data = decode(repeat('00', value), 'hex') WHERE value IS NOT NULL;
CREATE TABLE another_table(ref_id INTEGER, description TEXT, FOREIGN KEY(ref_id) REFERENCES test_table(id));
INSERT INTO another_table(ref_id, description) SELECT id, 'Test' FROM test_table WHERE value > 0;
key_bug_features: Assertion failure during update with zeroblob in debug build
